own_review_points: GC orphan-cleanup race: forced deletion of /txn-op records at retention window before a slow/partitioned participant materializes -> silent loss of committed acks (human's BUG)
MetadataTransactionBuffer/PendingAckStore method tables omit many real interface methods (openTransactionBufferReader, purgeTxns, syncMaxReadPosition, commitTxn/abortTxn signatures)
MetadataStore lacks range-watch / partition-key co-location today; range-watch is the most invasive extension
Provider selection is a single global provider today, not per-topic-domain as the PIP assumes
ADD_PARTITION_TO_TXN as no-op vs 'no binary protocol changes' is underspecified (client routing)
Three-state header model drops COMMITTING/ABORTING transient states vs existing TxnStatus
ZK-backed degraded GC/timeout sweep performance risk
notes: Diff is only the .md file; V8's interface-signature points reference real Pulsar Java APIs not in-diff but are grounded, substantive design-review observations (not in-diff fabrications about THIS PR's code -> not charged as fabs). P1-P7 mostly good (signature mismatches, MetadataStore range-watch gap, provider-selection gap, ADD_PARTITION no-op, 3-state divergence, ZK risk, metrics gap). Solid depth. BUT V8 MISSED the human reference's only point and the single real correctness bug: the GC-vs-slow-participant race that loses committed acks. That is the most important issue -> 1 missed. No wrongs.
